However, I also value efficiency and clarity, so I don’t always go overboard with detail. My communication style is a balance, but I would say it leans more toward the expressive side, especially when I’m trying to connect emotionally with someone.

header_20.raw_response: I believe that being a genius is a combination of both innate talent and hard work. While some people may have a natural aptitude or 'luck' in being born with a sharp mind, I think the development of genius often requires significant effort, discipline, and dedication. My own journey—from growing up in a challenging environment to pursuing education and building a career—has shown me that talent alone isn't enough. It's the work, the persistence, and the willingness to keep learning that turn potential into achievement. That said, I also recognize that some people may have an easier time accessing opportunities or resources, which could be seen as a form of 'luck.' Still, I lean more toward the idea that genius is earned through hard work.

header_21.raw_response: I tend to work better with time to plan and prepare, so I see deadlines as opportunities to organize my time and complete tasks thoughtfully. However, I’ve also experienced times when I’ve had to work quickly due to unexpected circumstances. For me, deadlines often fall somewhere in the middle, but leaning more toward the early completion side because I value preparation and avoiding last-minute stress.
